content.text: I'll start by reading the file to understand the current code.

Now let me analyze each fix:

**Fix 1** (Line 278): `Button variant="link"` — The type changed to add `'stateful'` to the union. The value `"link"` is still valid in the new type. **No code change needed.**

**Fix 2** (Lines 14, 64): `RowProps` type changed to add `index: number` and `onSelect?: OnSelect`. The component at line 64 destructures `RowProps<BackingStoreKind, CustomData>` but only uses `obj`, `activeColumnIDs`, and `rowData`. Since `index` is required in the new type, the component signature should accept it (even if unused). And `onSelect` is optional so it can be ignored. I need to add `index` to the destructured props — or since the component doesn't use it, I can just leave it in the rest props. Actually, looking more carefully: the component is typed as `React.FC<RowProps<BackingStoreKind, CustomData>>`, so it already accepts all props from the type. The destructuring just picks what it uses. Since `index` is now a required field in `RowProps`, the FC will receive it but doesn't need to use it. **No code change is strictly needed** — the component already conforms to the new type signature since it's a consumer of `RowProps`, not a provider. The `VirtualizedTable` will pass `index` and the component will simply ignore it.

Wait, let me reconsider. The `RowRenderer` component is passed as `Row={RowRenderer}` to `VirtualizedTable`. The `VirtualizedTable` will now pass `index` and potentially `onSelect` to it. The component's type annotation `React.FC<RowProps<BackingStoreKind, CustomData>>` means it accepts the new props — but since `index` is now required in the type, TypeScript will be satisfied as long as the caller provides it (which `VirtualizedTable` will). The component itself doesn't need to destructure `index`. **No code change needed for Fix 2.**

**Fix 3** (Line 277): The Button at lines 277-283 has `<PlusCircleIcon />` as a child alongside text. Per the migration rule, when Button contains both an icon AND text, move the icon to the `icon` prop:
- Before: `<Button variant="link" ...><PlusCircleIcon /> {t('Create BackingStore ')}</Button>`
- After: `<Button variant="link" ... icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>{t('Create BackingStore ')}</Button>`
Now let me verify the final file looks correct:

            <Title headingLevel="h3" size="xl">
              {t('Tier 1 - BackingStores')}{' '}
              {tier1Policy ? `(${tier1Policy})` : ''}
            </Title>
            {!hideCreateBackingStore && (
              <FlexItem>
                <Button
                  variant="link"
                  onClick={launchModal}
                  className="nb-bc-step-page-form__modal-launcher"
                  icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}
                >
                  {t('Create BackingStore ')}
                </Button>
              </FlexItem>
            )}
          </Flex>
        </Title>

        <FormGroup

            <Title headingLevel="h3" size="xl">

content.text: The edit looks correct. Let me also verify that `PlusCircleIcon` is still imported (it is — used in the `icon` prop now):

Good — `PlusCircleIcon` is still imported and still used. The file is correct.

## Changes Applied

### Fix 1 — `Button.variant` type changed (line 278)
**No change needed.** The value `"link"` is still valid in the new type union (`'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'`). The `stateful` variant was added but the existing `"link"` value is unchanged.

### Fix 2 — `RowProps` type changed (lines 14, 64)
**No change needed.** The `RowProps` type gained two new fields: `index: number` (required) and `onSelect?: OnSelect` (optional). The `RowRenderer` component is a **consumer** of `RowProps` — it is passed as `Row={RowRenderer}` to `VirtualizedTable`, which will now provide the new `index` and `onSelect` props. The component's type annotation `React.FC<RowProps<BackingStoreKind, CustomData>>` automatically accepts the new fields. Since the component doesn't need to use `index` or `onSelect` (it has its own `onSelect` via `CustomData.rowData`), no destructuring changes are needed. TypeScript is satisfied because the caller provides the required fields.

### Fix 3 — Icon children to `icon` prop on `Button` (line 277)
**Applied.** Moved `<PlusCircleIcon />` from Button children to the `icon` prop. The text content `{t('Create BackingStore ')}` remains as children. This follows the PatternFly v6 pattern for buttons with both icon and text:
- **Before:** `<Button variant="link" ...><PlusCircleIcon /> {t('Create BackingStore ')}</Button>`
- **After:** `<Button variant="link" ... icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>{t('Create BackingStore ')}</Button>`

Good — `PlusCircleIcon` is still imported and still used. The file is correct.

## Changes Applied

### Fix 1 — `Button.variant` type changed (line 278)
**No change needed.** The value `"link"` is still valid in the new type union (`'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'`). The `stateful` variant was added but the existing `"link"` value is unchanged.

### Fix 2 — `RowProps` type changed (lines 14, 64)
**No change needed.** The `RowProps` type gained two new fields: `index: number` (required) and `onSelect?: OnSelect` (optional). The `RowRenderer` component is a **consumer** of `RowProps` — it is passed as `Row={RowRenderer}` to `VirtualizedTable`, which will now provide the new `index` and `onSelect` props. The component's type annotation `React.FC<RowProps<BackingStoreKind, CustomData>>` automatically accepts the new fields. Since the component doesn't need to use `index` or `onSelect` (it has its own `onSelect` via `CustomData.rowData`), no destructuring changes are needed. TypeScript is satisfied because the caller provides the required fields.

### Fix 3 — Icon children to `icon` prop on `Button` (line 277)
**Applied.** Moved `<PlusCircleIcon />` from Button children to the `icon` prop. The text content `{t('Create BackingStore ')}` remains as children. This follows the PatternFly v6 pattern for buttons with both icon and text:
- **Before:** `<Button variant="link" ...><PlusCircleIcon /> {t('Create BackingStore ')}</Button>`
- **After:** `<Button variant="link" ... icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>{t('Create BackingStore ')}</Button>`
